name: Flan
recipeInstructions:
1. PREHEAT OVEN TO 350°F.
2. Warm a 1-quart souffle dish by placing it in the oven for 5 to 6 minutes.
3. Meanwhile, heat 1/2 cup granulated sugar in a saucepan over high heat until it begins to melt.
4. Cook, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon, until the sugar liquefies.
5. Reduce heat and cook, stirring constantly, until deep golden.
6. Remove from heat and pour caramel into the warmed souffle dish, turning the dish to coat the bottom and sides.
7. Invert the dish onto lightly buttered aluminum foil.
8. Cool.
9. Heat the milk, cream, and vanilla until hot; do not boil.
10. Remove from heat and beat the eggs and egg yolks in a large bowl until light colored.
11. Whisk in the remaining 3/4 cup sugar and beat until light and lemon-colored.
12. Stir in the orange juice.
13. Slowly whisk in the warm milk mixture.
14. Pour custard into the prepared dish.
15. Place the dish in a roasting pan and pour boiling water into the pan to come halfway up the sides of the dish.
16. Bake until a knife inserted in the center comes out fairly clean, about 1 hour. Do not let the water boil.
17. Cool on a wire rack; refrigerate, covered, overnight.
18. To unmold flan: Heat 1-inch water in a large skillet to boiling; turn off heat.
19. Dip a knife in water and run around the edge of the flan.
20. Dip the bottom of the souffle dish into water for a few seconds.
21. Place a serving dish over top and invert onto the dish.
22. To double the recipe, make it twice.
23. For individual custard cups, increase sugar for caramel to 1 cup.
